basic_details.job_title: JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 288 Posts
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Written Examination, Physical Standard Test, Physical Endurance Test, Document Verification
basic_details.short_information: The Jammu and Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) has released Advertisement Notification No. 05 of 2026, dated 06 July 2026, inviting online applications for 288 vacancies of Warder (UT Cadre) under the Home Department, J&K Prisons Department. Eligible candidates can apply online at www.jkssb.nic.in from 27 July 2026 to 25 August 2026. This article explains every important detail of the notification, including vacancy distribution, eligibility conditions, physical standards, exam pattern, fee structure, and the complete Apply Online procedure.

application_fee.details: The examination fee varies by category and must be paid online only, through net banking, credit card, or debit card. | Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances, nor adjusted against any other examination or selection. Applications submitted without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.

age_limit.details: Age eligibility is calculated as on 1 January 2026, and the permissible age band differs by category, as summarised below.

qualification.details: Minimum Matriculation (10th) and Maximum 10+2 from a recognised Board of School Education/Institution. Candidates must clear the Physical Standard Test and Physical Endurance Test as detailed below. Candidates declared qualified/shortlisted for Document Verification must produce relevant original certificates as proof of the prescribed educational qualification, strictly on or before the cut-off date, failing which their candidature will be cancelled. Candidates claiming an equivalent educational qualification must produce a relevant Equivalence Certificate or obtain equivalence from the competent authority as and when required.

vacancy_details.Category: Residents of Areas Adjoining Line of Actual Control/International Border (ALC/IB)

category_wise_breakdown.category: Residents of Areas Adjoining Line of Actual Control/International Border (ALC/IB)

selection_process: Gender Standard
Male Minimum Height: 5'-6"; Minimum Chest: 32–33.5"
Female Minimum Height: 5'-2"; Physically fit
Test Requirement
Long Race (Males) 1600 metres in 6½ minutes
Long Race (Females) 1000 metres in 6½ minutes
Push-ups (Males only) 20 (one cycle of up and down counted as one)
Shot Put (Females only, 4 Kgs) 14½ feet in three attempts
Age Long Race - Male (1600 Mtrs.) Push-ups (Male) Long Race - Female (1000 Mtrs.) Shot Put - Female (4 Kgs, 3 attempts)
Up to 30 years 6½ Minutes 20 6½ Minutes 14½ Feet
Above 30 to 40 years 7½ Minutes 16 7½ Minutes 12½ Feet
Above 40 years 8½ Minutes 12 8½ Minutes 10½ Feet
Selection for the post of Warder comprises a Written Examination followed by a Physical Standard Test
Physical Endurance Test
and Document Verification.
The examination will consist of Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions
set in the English language only. There will be negative marking for each wrong answer to the extent of one-fourth (1/4) of the marks assigned to that question.
Candidates are shortlisted for the Physical Standard Test on the basis of the written test
at six times the total number of vacancies to be filled in each category. The prescribed physical standards are:
Candidates who meet the physical standards must undergo the Physical Endurance Test
which is qualifying in nature and carries no marks.
Note: For this purpose

the age of the Ex-Serviceman is his actual age as on 1 January 2026. All Ex-Servicemen must also pass the written test and meet the prescribed medical standards for direct recruits. The Physical Standard Test and Physical Endurance Test are both qualifying in nature only.
Selected candidates
with reference to the vacancies
will be medically examined to assess their physical and medical fitness.
how_to_apply: Candidates must fill the application form in online mode only through the official website; no other mode of application will be accepted. Detailed instructions and the Online Portal link will be made available separately on the Board's website.

Only one online application is allowed per candidate for the post of the same item number; if multiple applications are detected, the Board will consider the latest one, and appearing in the examination more than once under the same item number will lead to cancellation of candidature and debarment.
Requests for modification or change in preferences relating to cadre and department, once filled, will not be entertained under any circumstances.
Applications with blurred or illegible photograph/signature will be summarily rejected.

In-service candidates must submit a printout of the filled online application along with a duly filled, signed, and stamped certificate through the proper channel (Head of Department/Designated Authority), to reach the Board before the last date of Document Verification.

Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the closing date, since heavy website traffic near the deadline may cause disconnection or inability to log in to the online application portal.
important_instructions: Misconduct/Malpractice: Any irregularity, misconduct, malpractice, or misbehaviour at any stage of the selection process will lead to debarment from JKSSB examinations for a period deemed appropriate, besides other penal action under extant rules and laws. Security Screening: Submission of the application form implies willingness to undergo screening measures put in place by the Board, including physical frisking, Door Frame Metal Detector (DFMD) scanning, biometric capturing, facial recognition screening, and Aadhaar authentication. No Correction Facility: Requests for change or correction in any particulars of the application form, once submitted, will not be entertained under any circumstances. Finality of Decision: The decision of the Board on all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, conduct of examinations, allotment of examination centres, and preparation of the merit list will be final and binding.

rows.details: Jammu and Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB)
Written Examination, Physical Standard Test, Physical Endurance Test, Document Verification

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: This section lists every key date a candidate needs to track, from notification release to the closing date, so applicants do not miss any stage of the process.

content_sections.heading: JKSSB Warder Vacancy Details 2026 (Category-Wise Break-Up)
content_sections.text: The vacancies for the post of Warder are distributed across Open Merit and various reserved categories, as per the indent received from the Home Department. Item Number 92 covers the post of Warder under the J&K Prisons Department, sanctioned against Indent HOME-Jail/122/2021-01-HOME [CC34193] dated 10.02.2026, followed up on 12.06.2026.

rows.category: Residents of Areas Adjoining Line of Actual Control/International Border (ALC/IB)

content_sections.heading: Age Limit for JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Age eligibility is calculated as on 1 January 2026 , and the permissible age band differs by category, as summarised below.

rows.max_age_limit_years: Actual service period is deducted from actual age; if the resultant age does not exceed the maximum age limit prescribed for the post by more than 3 years, the age condition is deemed satisfied. Candidate should not be born after 01.01.2008.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification for JKSSB Warder Post
content_sections.text: Minimum Matriculation ( 10th ) and Maximum 10 +2 from a recognised Board of School Education/Institution. Candidates must clear the Physical Standard Test and Physical Endurance Test as detailed below. Candidates declared qualified/shortlisted for Document Verification must produce relevant original certificates as proof of the prescribed educational qualification, strictly on or before the cut-off date, failing which their candidature will be cancelled. Candidates claiming an equivalent educational qualification must produce a relevant Equivalence Certificate or obtain equivalence from the competent authority as and when required.

content_sections.heading: Domicile Requirement
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the advertised posts must be Domicile(s) of the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir and must possess a valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Competent Authority in the prescribed format on or before the cut-off date, i.e. 25 August 2026 (unless extended).

content_sections.heading: JKSSB Exam Pattern and Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection for the post of Warder comprises a Written Examination followed by a Physical Standard Test, Physical Endurance Test, and Document Verification. The examination will consist of Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions , set in the English language only . There will be negative marking for each wrong answer to the extent of one-fourth (1/4) of the marks assigned to that question. Candidates are shortlisted for the Physical Standard Test on the basis of the written test, at six times the total number of vacancies to be filled in each category. The prescribed physical standards are: Candidates who meet the physical standards must undergo the Physical Endurance Test, which is qualifying in nature and carries no marks. Note: For this purpose, the age of the Ex-Serviceman is his actual age as on 1 January 2026. All Ex-Servicemen must also pass the written test and meet the prescribed medical standards for direct recruits. The Physical Standard Test and Physical Endurance Test are both qualifying in nature only. Selected candidates, with reference to the vacancies, will be medically examined to assess their physical and medical fitness.

rows.requirement: 20 (one cycle of up and down counted as one)

content_sections.heading: JKSSB Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The examination fee varies by category and must be paid online only, through net banking, credit card, or debit card. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances, nor adjusted against any other examination or selection. Applications submitted without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy in JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Reservation for the advertised posts is applicable as per the category-wise break-up conveyed by the Indenting Department and the Jammu & Kashmir Reservation Act/Rules, as amended from time to time. Candidates seeking reserved category consideration must possess a valid Category Certificate strictly as on the cut-off date, uploaded at the time of application; no further opportunity to upload documents will be given later. Reservation certificates must be in the prescribed format as per the relevant Act/S.O.; certificates in any other format will not be entertained. Horizontal Reservation for Ex-Servicemen (ESM) is applicable as per extant rules, for posts up to Pay Level-6E (35,900–1,13,500). An Ex-Serviceman who has already availed Horizontal Reservation on joining a post cannot avail this benefit again for subsequent employment; an affidavit sworn before a 1st Class Judicial Magistrate is required at the time of document scrutiny. ESM candidates must produce a Certificate in Form-XIV, duly signed by the competent authority, as provided in Rule 18 of the Jammu and Kashmir Reservation Rules, 2005. Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) are not eligible for this post.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for JKSSB Online Application
content_sections.text: Keep the following documents ready in scanned format before starting the application, as uploading them is required at the application stage or Document Verification stage. Matriculation certificate (for verification of Name, Date of Birth, Father's Name and Mother's Name) Valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Competent Authority Category certificate (SC/ST-1/ST-2/OBC/EWS/RBA/ALC-IB), if applicable, in the prescribed format Form-XIV certificate for Ex-Servicemen (ESM), if applicable Bonafide Certificate (for candidates with a degree from Universities/Colleges outside the UT of J&K and other than Central Universities) Live photograph and signature (Size: 10 KB to 20 KB) Category certificate in PDF format (Max size: 100 KB) Qualification/experience certificates, self-attested, in PDF format (Max size: 100 KB) Two recent passport-size colour photographs (not older than 6 months, with date mentioned) and original valid Photo-ID proof (Aadhaar/e-Aadhaar printout, Voter ID, Driving Licence, PAN Card, Passport, School/College/University I-Card, or Employer ID Card) – to be carried to the examination hall and Document Verification

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must fill the application form in online mode only through the official website; no other mode of application will be accepted. Detailed instructions and the Online Portal link will be made available separately on the Board's website. Only one online application is allowed per candidate for the post of the same item number; if multiple applications are detected, the Board will consider the latest one, and appearing in the examination more than once under the same item number will lead to cancellation of candidature and debarment. Requests for modification or change in preferences relating to cadre and department, once filled, will not be entertained under any circumstances. Applications with blurred or illegible photograph/signature will be summarily rejected. In-service candidates must submit a printout of the filled online application along with a duly filled, signed, and stamped certificate through the proper channel (Head of Department/Designated Authority), to reach the Board before the last date of Document Verification. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the closing date, since heavy website traffic near the deadline may cause disconnection or inability to log in to the online application portal.

content_sections.heading: Document Verification (DV)
content_sections.text: Shortlisted candidates must appear for Document Verification with original documents as well as self-attested photocopies of each document/certificate/online application form. Candidates must possess the prescribed academic qualification and documents such as Birth Certificate, Domicile Certificate, and Category Certificate, all legally valid on or before the cut-off date. Failure to appear before the Document Verification Committee, or failure to produce original documents on the scheduled date, will result in the candidate not being considered for selection. No TA/DA will be paid for participation in the written test or Document Verification. Two recent passport-size colour photographs and a valid original ID proof must be produced at the time of Document Verification.

content_sections.heading: Other Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Misconduct/Malpractice: Any irregularity, misconduct, malpractice, or misbehaviour at any stage of the selection process will lead to debarment from JKSSB examinations for a period deemed appropriate, besides other penal action under extant rules and laws. Security Screening: Submission of the application form implies willingness to undergo screening measures put in place by the Board, including physical frisking, Door Frame Metal Detector (DFMD) scanning, biometric capturing, facial recognition screening, and Aadhaar authentication. No Correction Facility: Requests for change or correction in any particulars of the application form, once submitted, will not be entertained under any circumstances. Finality of Decision: The decision of the Board on all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, conduct of examinations, allotment of examination centres, and preparation of the merit list will be final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies have been announced in JKSSB Advertisement No. 05 of 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 288 vacancies for the post of Warder (UT Cadre) have been announced under the Home Department, J&K Prisons Department.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts on 27 July 2026 and closes on 25 August 2026, which is also the cut-off date for eligibility.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the post of Warder?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Matriculation as the minimum qualification, with 10+2 as the maximum qualification, from a recognised Board of School Education/Institution.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for JKSSB Warder Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Age is calculated as on 1 January 2026. The maximum age limit is 40 years for Open Merit candidates and 43 years for SC, ST-1, ST-2, RBA, ALC/IB, EWS, and OBC candidates, subject to the "not born after 01.01.2008" condition.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs 700 for general/other categories and Rs 600 for SC, ST-1, ST-2, and EWS candidates, payable only through online mode (net banking, credit card, or debit card).

faq.question: Q6. Is domicile of Jammu and Kashmir mandatory to apply?
faq.answer: Yes, only Domicile(s) of the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir holding a valid Domicile Certificate issued on or before the cut-off date (25 August 2026) are eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Warder post?
faq.answer: Selection comprises a Written Examination (objective-type, English only, with negative marking), followed by a qualifying Physical Standard Test, a qualifying Physical Endurance Test, and Document Verification.

faq.question: Q8. Are Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) eligible for this post?
faq.answer: No, the notification specifically states that Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) are not eligible for the post of Warder.

faq.question: Q9. What are the Physical Standard Test requirements for male and female candidates?
faq.answer: Male candidates need a minimum height of 5'-6" and minimum chest of 32–33.5", while female candidates need a minimum height of 5'-2" and must be physically fit.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates make corrections to their application after submission?
faq.answer: No, requests for change or correction in any particulars of the application form, once submitted, will not be entertained under any circumstances, so candidates should fill the form carefully before final submission.
